delegation_capability.evidence_ref: Sixteen fresh built-in default reviews completed. After round 16 remediation, both direct and relayed spawn attempts for dispatch 20260715T024254Z--HCM-0-4--fresh-result-identity-bridge-recovery-review failed with agent thread limit reached; old-reviewer reuse and self-review are prohibited.

objective: Freeze HCM-0.4 crate ownership, typed SDK operation/DTO boundaries, JSON/Tauri transport parity, transitional Substrate process integration, permanent published-Rust adoption, and proof/deletion gates without implementing Rust.
work_completed: Grounded HCM-0.4 against the exact completed HCM-0.3 handoff and live Handbook/Substrate repository truth.
Documented the target crate graph, 50-operation typed SDK catalog, DTO/schema ownership, CLI JSON and Tauri parity, publication DAG, transitional bridge, permanent Rust boundary, and deletion gates.
Completed sixteen different fresh default read-only reviews and parent remediations through the committed-result identity and commit-uncertain bridge boundary.
Applied the round-16 remediation and created a replayable seventeenth review dispatch bound to the exact seven-document subject.
Ran the full documentation/schema/link/archive/handoff proof wall and staged GitNexus change detection before the review-pending checkpoint commit.

decisions.summary: Keep HCM-0.4 documentation/design-only; no Rust, Cargo, CLI, Tauri, or Substrate implementation is authorized.
Use one typed SDK operation/DTO/schema boundary with thin JSON, Tauri, and transitional process adapters.
decisions.authority_effect: freezes target ownership only; runtime gates remain open

decisions.summary: Keep the process bridge transitional and crates.io Rust consumption permanent, with separate proof and deletion gates.
Do not declare the slice complete without a different fresh clean reviewer over the exact current subject.

blockers.resume_condition: Start a new top-level orchestration context with fresh built-in default-agent capacity and execute the exact pending dispatch before making any completion claim.

resume.objective: Resume HCM-0.4 at the mandatory different-fresh review wall; do not start another slice.
resume.condition: A new top-level task has capacity to allocate a fresh built-in default reviewer.
allowed_scope: Execute the exact pending round-17 dispatch with a fresh built-in default read-only reviewer.
If findings remain, remediate only HCM-0.4 and dispatch a different fresh reviewer.
If clean, rerun the full proof wall, create a reviewed primary completion commit, and write a completed parent handoff/ledger closeout.
prohibited_scope: Do not reuse any prior HCM-0.4 reviewer or self-approve.
Do not implement Rust or modify Cargo, CLI, Tauri, or Substrate runtime code.
Do not start HCM-0.5, HCM-0.6, or any sibling slice automatically.
Do not promote SDK/JSON/Tauri/Substrate/publication runtime gates without real-path proof.
acceptance_criteria: The exact pending subject manifest replays from repository state.
A different fresh default reviewer returns clean, or every new finding is remediated and re-reviewed by another different fresh reviewer.
The full proof wall and staged GitNexus change detection pass.
A completed v1.2 parent handoff binds the final clean review and reviewed primary commit.
stop_conditions: If fresh built-in delegation remains unavailable, write another capability-unavailable true-stop record rather than reusing a reviewer or self-approving.
Stop on any scope expansion beyond HCM-0.4 or any request to implement Rust.

chat_summary: HCM-0.4 is not complete. Sixteen fresh reviews were remediated through committed-result and bridge recovery semantics, but the required different-fresh round-17 reviewer could not be allocated because the runtime hit its agent thread limit. The exact review dispatch and review-pending checkpoint are durable; no Rust or runtime gate changed.
